entry.title: Re: App UI5 in workzone show loading screen never end
entry.content:
@thuythai 

Are there any errors in the cloud connector logs? It's possible that specific services are blocked by the cloud connector because they are not included in the allowed paths.

entry.title: Re: Connectivity guaranteed with Cloud Integration versio...
entry.content:
Hello @Takuya_Nezu,

the Cloud side is never behind so that it will always support the latest Cloud Connector versions. SAP note 3302250 describes the support strategy of Cloud Connector so that you should upgrade the Cloud Connector fast enough to comply to the rules. We strongly recommend to always plan the upgrade to the latest patch release very fast, for switching to the latest feature release you can take a little more time, but it should not be delayed for too long.
Once the version is no longer supported, there is no guarantee that the interoperability with the cloud side is still working. 

Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Inside SAP: Discover How SAP’s IT Drives Transformation and Innovation
entry.content:
We go through a great amount of detail making dinner or lunch for guests: Not too salty, about enough sauce, every ingredient balanced to the recipe’s perfect recommendation. There is usually an air of excitement about it in the house. But would you eat your own food and enjoy it? Technology has the same tenets, that charity begins at home.  

The TechTalk series under the umbrella of Discover SAP are a joint event by SAP’s IT and SAP Global User Groups organizations. They tell the stories of how we at SAP eat our own food, i.e. use our own technology and empower SAP's workforce with innovative solutions. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series gives the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into the inner workings of the digital landscape. We shine a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. We leverage the power of storytelling and show how we work at SAP. 

Mark your calendars and make sure to join starting May 16th : We will be launching a webcast of YouTube Series TechTalks with Discover SAP content on the SAP Community Youtube Channel, offering you a behind-the-scenes look into SAP's kitchen. The first session will be led by the charismatic Dr. Oliver Gutzeit, who is the Senior Director of SAP’s Global Experience Technology unit within SAP’s internal IT. In the series, you can delve into the intricate workings of SAP's IT unit and pick into the engine driving the company's transformation journey. Inspirational, engaging, and hands-on, the Discover SAP series will pull back the curtains, offering insights into how SAP operates and its invaluable expertise. Through authentic stories and expert commentary, the first session will treat you to a preview of what is in store at SAP Sapphire 2024, where Discover SAP will take center stage. The next sessions will continue after SAP Sapphire 2024 giving you the opportunity to dive into topics surrounding Strategy & Innovation, AI, Finance, Analytics, HR and more.  

Visit the Discover SAP webpage, register for the series, join the sessions, and come along for the ride. Discover SAP isn’t just a show; it's an experience. So, let’s dive into the world of SAP together!

See you there!

entry.title: Publicize original date/ seniority date/ service date from terminated employment.
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

In one of the existing blog Propagating Original Start Date to Rehire with new... - SAP Community we have seen the setup to fetch the original start date from previous employment details. But many of us including me faced challenge with the given Business Rule.

In this blog I will share the setup I have applied for our client requirement to auto populate the employment details dates from terminated record while performing Rehire/ Rehire with New Employment:

Steps applied in system:

- Create new MDF table and link with intelligent services > integration (refer the above blog)

Solution outcome: - after terminating an employee, User Id, Person Id, Original Start Date, Seniority Date & Service Date record will be auto populated in mdf object:

- Now we have to place this in a Business rule, so that whenever Rehire/ Rehire with New employment action performed from 'Add new Employee' hire form. The saved Terminated employees' dates should Auto populate in the dates 'employment details' portlet after submission of the hire form.

-  onSave rule for the Employment Details portlet to default the original start date, seniority date & service date from the lookup table using the PersonId field as query parameter:

- Save the rule in Manage Business Configuration- Employee Central> HRIS Elements> employmentInfo: 

- To access the MDF object data grant permission:

Hope this rule will help in solutioning the requirement. Thanks for reading.

Regards,

Papia Basu

entry.title: How Defense and Security Organizations can Easily Migrate to the Cloud with RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Figure 1: Example picture

 

1) Introduction

­­

In the recent years, cloud computing has been among the most important IT trends, and it will continue to do so within the next decade. The leading technology consulting firm, Gartner, published various insightful statistics about the technology and its importance that highlight the importance for both private enterprises and public organizations. For one thing, as the below graph shows, the market for public cloud services will grow to $1 trillion by 2026. This relates to a growth rate of more than 100% when compared to the year 2021 and even marks the highest growth rate of all existing IT markets.

 

Figure 2: Forecast for public cloud services growth by Gartner

 

This strong trend towards cloud computing is also reflected in a Gartner survey among 367 enterprises . As you can see in the chart below, more than 50% of the survey participants in 2022 think that cloud technology is either indispensable or heavily impactful. When asked about the year 2027, even 90% believe that this will be the case.

Figure 3: Gartner cloud computing survey

 

Furthermore, 50% of surveyed organizations will use industry cloud platforms to accelerate their digital business initiatives in 2027, according to Gartner. In contrast, less than 5% of the participants gave this answer in 2022.

 

2) The Importance of Cloud Computing for the Defense and Security Industry

 

Cloud services are also of high importance for Defense and Security (D&S) organizations, like armed forces, aid organizations or emergency organizations. A recent study financed by the European Defense Agency shows that cloud computing can be applied to both the strategic level and the field level of military organizations. Furthermore, cloud technology can support European armed forces in achieving information superiority against its adversaries. The advantages of cloud technology apply to emergency organizations, like police forces, in a similar manner. Here cloud technology does not only improve the collaboration within and across police organizations, but also helps to complete criminal investigations faster. Fire departments can benefit from the technology as well. Examples for this include cost reductions, access to innovative IT services, a higher reliability of IT services or improved cybersecurity. Rescue service organizations, in turn, can profit from cloud computing through a better and faster handling of medical data. In this way, rescue chains in general and the planning of medical treatments can be improved significantly, which in the end can help to save people’s lives.

 

3) What is RISE with SAP?

 

Figure 4: Introductory video to RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP is a holistic package for customers, which want to transform their existing on-premises SAP systems into the cloud. RISE with SAP thereby not only includes SAP’s top-of-the-range software solution, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, but also various connected services, such as, cloud infrastructure and security as well as related migration and modernization programs. Businesses and organizations, which are not yet SAP customers can instead use GROW with SAP. GROW with SAP is an offer, that is specifically designed for new customers and small and midsized companies and organizations. But let us now take a closer look what specifically RISE with SAP offers for existing customers.

 

Figure 5: RISE with SAP overview graphic

 

The RISE package generally makes system transformations easier and allows customers to profit from all of the latest innovations from SAP. The key components of RISE – as the above graphic shows – are:

1) Cloud Solutions

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition allows you to optimize processes in an unprecedented way. The Cloud ERP solution in this regard covers all common business functions of Defense and Security organizations like, for instance, procurement, human resource management or military planning and operations. Additionally, RISE with SAP includes packages, like the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for app development, data management, automation and real-time analytics and SAP Signavio for process mining. The RISE solution beyond this also includes diverse innovative and helpful SAP Business Artificial Intelligence functions and features, that allow your organization to get better insights and save valuable time and money. What is more, the RISE program enables D&S organizations to use our holistic sustainability software, SAP Sustainability Solutions. These solutions not only allow organizations to monitor and manage sustainability and environmental issues, but also to ensure the safety of their employees at their workplaces.

 

2) Cloud Infrastructure and Security

RISE with SAP is built on the foundation of state-of-the-art IT infrastructure and security. In this regard, our customers can benefit from a very modern cloud infrastructure, that helps to ensure the highest possible system performance and availability of services around the clock. Furthermore, D&S organizations can profit from simple and effective service level agreements (SLAs), that guarantee a 99.7% availability as well as regular maintenance of our SAP cloud solutions. Moreover, our RISE with SAP offering is based on the highest standards for security and data protection. This not only entails regular security updates, but also the ability of permanently monitoring the system with regards to performance, compliance and potential threats.

 

3) Migration and Modernization

Our RISE with SAP cloud package aims to make the migration from on-premises systems to modern cloud systems as simple as possible. SAP for this created an easy, guided implementation process, that is based on the SAP Activate methodology. This methodology allows our customers to:

  • Gain an in-depth understanding of the extensive capabilities of cloud ERP.
  • Receive expert guidance to strategically plan and prepare for a smooth migration.
  • Discover new avenues to craft detailed implementation plans across multiple workstreams.
  • Seamlessly progress through all six implementation stages with clarity and precision.
  • Tap into a vast partner ecosystem for enhanced support and collaboration.
  • Access comprehensive assistance, that is available in more than 100 countries worldwide.

 

4) The Key Advantages of RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP unites all advantages of cloud computing technology with the well-known advantages of SAP software solutions. The combination of the two technologies thereby even enabled SAP to create its most advanced ERP products so far, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The main advantages of RISE with SAP are:

High scalability and usage-based pricing

Our cloud solutions allow your D&S organization to easily scale your SAP systems, in a needs-based manner. This is not limited to a certain part of your organization, but can be applied across your entire global operations. The high scalability thereby not only facilitates your organization’s IT processes, but also pays off financially due to the “pay only what you need approach”.

 

Better collaboration

SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows for a better collaboration among your workforce. Your employees are enabled to work jointly on tasks, communicate directly and share information. This can be done for instance with the SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, a versatile tool for process collaboration. Furthermore, our cloud ERP solutions also enable our clients to better collaborate with their stakeholders, such as suppliers.

 

Process automatization and access to diverse innovative functions and features

The RISE with SAP offering entails access to various innovative platforms, functions, and features as well as to a variety of useful partner solutions. Examples for this are the SAP Business Technology Platform or SAP Business Artificial Intelligence, two solutions, that were designed to complement each other perfectly. The BTP, on the one hand, is a platform, that bundles services like data management and analytics, application development, automation, and integration as well as artificial intelligence in one environment. The platform is thereby also helping to accelerate the transformation to the cloud significantly. SAP Business AI, on the other hand, is an umbrella term for all available AI solutions from SAP. Good examples for this are SAP Joule, a Generative Artificial Intelligence-based copilot within SAP applications. SAP Joule can answer diverse questions, guide the user through the application or even solve various tasks in an automated manner. Other examples for SAP Business AI include the automatization of processes, such as guided buying or automatically generated job descriptions.

 

5) Migration Options

 

D&S organizations, which are already using SAP solutions and want to migrate their systems to the cloud can choose among two principal migration options:

1) Migrate from SAP ERP

D&S organizations, that use SAP ERP and want to migrate to the state-of-the-art SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition can profit from a guided transformation process. This process allows the organizations to make use of diverse best practices for the migration of their systems and data throughout all phases of the transformation journey. Principal advantages of this concept are that customers are enabled to maintain system configurations just like their data sets easily in the new cloud environment.

 

2) Migrate from SAP S/4HANA

The migration from an existing on-premises SAP S/4HANA system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is even less complex. The transition to the cloud here works very frictionless, which means that – in most cases – customers can just transform their systems and data centers to the new S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition environment.

 

3) Alternative for new customers: GROW with SAP

As already mentioned above, SAP recommends new customers the tailored cloud transformation program GROW with SAP. GROW with SAP allows for a fast and predictable implementation of the SAP cloud systems and includes most of the advantages of the RISE offering. Unlike the RISE with SAP package, the GROW solution relies on a Public Cloud architecture in the form of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

6) Security Features of RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP is built on a comprehensive security architecture, that enables our customers not only to work safely, but also to protect their data regardless from where and in which local circumstance they work. The security architecture comprises enterprise-grade security features and standards, like:

  • Physical access control
  • User authentication and authorization functions
  • Advanced functions for the function and user-based separation of data
  • Data encryption
  • Automated back-ups and protection from data losses (data recovery)
  • Data encryption features
  • Full compliance with data protection guidelines like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)

- Comprehensive protection of client data

- Auditing functions

- Integrated capabilities for deleting personal data

  • Sovereign Cloud solutions for organizations with higher security and data-protection requirements, including:

- Use of data centers, that are located in the resident country or region of the respective company or organization

- Data being solely stored and processed within the sovereign borders of the country or region

- Corresponding proof of national and international security certifications and attestations

- Adherence to high security standards throughout all steps

- Increased security requirements for employees

 

7) Cloud Solutions with Advanced Security Features by SAP NS2

 

For companies and organizations, which have even higher cybersecurity requirements, SAP is offering cloud solutions with advanced cyber security measures. SAP therefore established its own independent subsidiary called SAP National Security Services (SAP NS2). SAP NS2 is mainly focused on the US, Canadian and Australian market, where it, among other things, is responsible for the adherence of SAP products to diverse security and regulatory requirements. The focus of SAP NS2 thereby lies on ultra-secure cloud solutions for highly regulated industries like D&S organizations, but also for actors with such needs within other industries. This includes highest level of security standards, like the US Department of Defense Impact Level 4 (DoD IL4) requirements just like the US Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) certification. The company’s product portfolio includes:

  1. SAP Secure Cloud Services and Products
    • SAP NS2 offers a great range of SAP cloud products and solutions with enhanced security standards. The product portfolio includes ERP-solutions, like SAP S/4HANA, as well as diverse solutions for functional areas, like Supply Chain Management, Finance, Human Resource Management or Spend Management. In addition, NS2, with the SAP Business Technology Platform, also offers diverse automation, analytics, data management and Artificial Intelligence solutions.        
  2. Professional Services
    • SAP NS2 offers its customers a variety of Professional Services, that range from cloud transformation over advisory services to implementation services.                                                      
  3. Support
    • SAP NS2 permanently supports its customers with a highly specialized customer service team, which answers any kinds of questions or issue resolution.                                                                   
  4. Mission Solutions 
    • SAP NS2 offers a variety of additional services under its brand DataCTRL, that allow D&S customers to securely manage and catalog data across their organization. DataCTRL thereby enables D&S organizations to perform a better and faster data handling, which, for instance, can speed up the migration from on-premises to cloud systems.

 

Key Takeaways

 

All in all, this blog entry has shown that RISE with SAP brings significant benefits for Defense and Security organizations, that shouldn’t be ignored. This includes advantages, such as, process automatization just like better collaboration or access to the latest functions and features. What the blog has also shown is that cloud technology, like RISE with SAP, can make all types of D&S organizations more efficient and successful, and here even allows them to strengthen themselves within their core competencies. Although the security of cloud solutions is typically a rather controversial issue among IT responsibles, it has been shown in the blog that SAP’s cloud solutions are built on the foundation of a strong security architecture. This architecture includes various security functions and features like automated backups, encryption, or access control functions. For organizations with even higher cybersecurity requirements, SAP can refer its customers to its specialized subsidiary, SAP NS2. SAP NS2 offers ultra-secure cloud solutions with the highest possible security certifications for highly regulated industries, like Defense and Security. Beyond that, our customers can expect numerous further innovations for the future from SAP, which includes Generative AI functions just like the stepwise integration of SAP’s intelligent copilot, Joule, into SAP applications.  

 

Product Tour for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

 

If you are interested in learning more about RISE with SAP and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and its advantages for the different operational processes in the following product tour:

Figure 6: Link to the RISE with SAP product tour

 

Sources

 

Accelerate Digital Transformation, Enable Compliance, and Mitigate Risk with Sovereign Cloud Capabilities from SAP. (2023, September). SAP.

Breaking the cloud barrier in aerospace and defense. (2022). Accenture.

‘Combat cloud’: EDA study shows benefits of cloud computing for EU militaries. (2024, January 24). European Defense Agency.

Digitalisation of Defence in NATO and the EU: Making European Defence Fit for the Digital Age. (2023, August 10). IISS.

Healthcare Cloud Computing: Meaning, Advantages, and Implementation Pitfalls. (2023, August 16). IEEE.

How fire departments can extinguish money, space, and data loss with the cloud. (2020, May 11). ZDNET.

Military Cloud Computing Market. (2023, September). MarketsandMarkets.

RISE with SAP Overview. (2024). SAP.

SAP National Security Services Subsidiary. (2024). SAP NS2.

The Future of Cloud Computing in 2027. (2022). Gartner.

Transition to SAP S/4HANA. (2024). SAP.

Why using the cloud is about more than just efficient storage. (2022, September 2). POLICE1.

entry.title: Inside SAP: Discover How SAP’s IT Drives Transformation and Innovation
entry.content:
We go through a great amount of detail making dinner or lunch for guests: Not too salty, about enough sauce, every ingredient balanced to the recipe’s perfect recommendation. There is usually an air of excitement about it in the house. But would you eat your own food and enjoy it? Technology has the same tenets, that charity begins at home.  

The TechTalk series under the umbrella of Discover SAP are a joint event by SAP’s IT and SAP Global User Groups organizations. They tell the stories of how we at SAP eat our own food, i.e. use our own technology and empower SAP's workforce with innovative solutions. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series gives the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into the inner workings of the digital landscape. We shine a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. We leverage the power of storytelling and show how we work at SAP. 

Mark your calendars and make sure to join starting May 16th : We will be launching a webcast of YouTube Series TechTalks with Discover SAP content on the SAP Community Youtube Channel, offering you a behind-the-scenes  look into SAP's kitchen.The first session will be led by the charismatic Dr. Oliver Gutzeit, who is the Senior Director of SAP’s Global Experience Technology unit within SAP’ internal IT. In the series, you can delve into the intricate workings of SAP's IT unit and pick into the engine driving the company's transformation journey. Inspirational, engaging, and hands-on, the Discover SAP series will pull back the curtains, offering insights into how SAP operates and its invaluable expertise. Through authentic stories and expert commentary, the first session will treat you to a preview of what is in store at SAP Sapphire 2024, where Discover SAP will take center stage. The next sessions will continue after SAP Sapphire 2024 giving you the opportunity to dive into topics surrounding Strategy & Innovation, AI, Finance, Analytics, HR and more.  

Visit the Discover SAP webpage,  register for the series, join the sessions, and come along for the ride. Discover SAP isn’t just a show; it's an experience. So, let’s dive into the world of SAP together!

See you there!

entry.title: Boost Employee Satisfaction and Performance with SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing
entry.content:
Discover how the SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing Software Solution Allows Defense and Security Organizations to Monitor and Manage the Welfare of their Personnel in a Holistic Way

Figure 1: Example illustration

 

1) Thematic Background

 

Employees are the most important asset of almost every organization. This is even more true for Defense and Security (D&S) organizations, like armed forces, emergency organizations or relief organizations. The personnel of these organizations is typically confronted with high stress situations, high physical strains, and often times also long working hours. What is more, certain employees undergo life threatening situations throughout their career, or experience situations of high psychological stress during missions. Another important aspect of organizational health and wellbeing is physical fitness and injury prevention. What matters in this context is not only having the necessary fitness level to accomplish tasks and missions, but especially also the positive long-term effect on personal health. The importance of organizational health and wellbeing can be further underlined by different statistical data. According to a survey among first responders 85% of them have expected symptoms related to mental health conditions. Another survey shows that emergency responders think of committing suicide twice as often than average adults. Additionally, a survey conducted by the British National Police Wellbeing Service indicates that 69.6% of the British police officers and 56% of police staff have high levels of fatigue, and around 30% even very high levels. However, initiatives that want to promote wellbeing are not limited to the D&S organizations itself. The Firefighters Charity organization in Great Britain is a good example for a private institution, that is dedicated to the wellbeing of British fire fighters. Their services include telephone or online counselling, guided relaxation or reset programs for fire fighters, which are experiencing health problems.

 

2) The Impact of Health and Wellbeing Problems

 

Figure 2: Health and wellbeing problems can pose serious threats to D&S organizations

 

Wellbeing and health issues do not only harm the affected people, but also their employers. D&S organizations in this context typically experience the following difficulties:

1) An Inability to Identify Mental Trauma and Stress

Many D&S organizations find it difficult to recognize and measure stress and potential mental traumas among their staff.

 

2) Rising Absences of Employees

Employees that are experiencing health problems show more days of sick leave than average workforces.

 

3) Rising Employee Attrition

When the physical and psychological burdens become too great for staff members, they often decide to leave their employer.

 

4) Real risk to the Delivery of Service

With extremely high fluctuation rates of up to 30%, Defense and Security organizations start to be confronted with a real risk to the successful delivery of their service.

 

According to a study published by the nonprofit organization RAND the attrition rate after three years of new recruits of the US military amounts to 29.7% for the US Army and 23.6% for the US Navy. This high labor turnover rates do not only harm the operational readiness of the military, but also lead to significant knowledge loss and costs. The author of the study in this context estimates the turnover expenses for the US Military to up to $479mn for the US Army and to up to $220mn for the US Navy. He also emphasizes in that regard, that the costs for one single enlistment ranges from $11,000 to $20,000. What is more, D&S organizations do also need to consider additional costs for personnel search and selection, onboarding, and training. A similar development can also be seen for emergency services organizations. The ambulance service organizations in the United States of America, for instance, have an overall turnover rate in the year 2021 of between 21% for supervisors and 36% for full-time emergency medical technicians. By contrast, dedicated frontline forces like the British Columbian Wildfire Service in Canada reported a turnover rate of about 25% in 2022. Although there are of course also other reasons why people leave a company or organizations, it is unquestionable that health and wellbeing problems are among the most important ones.

 

3) How Organizations are Trying to Approach the Problem

    

Figure 3: Example illustration

 

Luckily, this problem has not remained unrecognized by the employers. The British Ministry of Defense, for instance, has successfully addressed it by offering stress management courses or access to medical support. Corresponding offers for individual military units, the soldiers’ families, women, or veterans have also been successfully established by the Ministry. Emergency organizations have similarly approached the issue during the recent years. The British National Police, for instance, has created wellbeing solutions like a peer support for wellbeing, wellbeing dogs, or even wellbeing vans, that tour through the country for raising awareness about the topic. However, nowadays many organizations still do not have implemented comparable health and wellbeing solutions for multiple reasons, such as budget constraint, the highly decentralized orientation of their organization or simply a lack of awareness. But this is exactly where SAP wants to come into play with its holistic software solutions for Health and Wellbeing.

 

4) The SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing Solution

 

Figure 4: Example illustration

 

We at SAP are a company with more than 100,000 employees worldwide, and it is absolutely clear for us that our organizational performance depends on the performance of each employee.  We for this reason recognized a long time ago how important it is to have a healthy and happy workforce, which is why we created the SAP Solution for Employee Health & Wellbeing. With this software solutions we want to enable our customers to run their organizations in a better, more efficient way, that enables its employees to unfold their full potential while also achieving a better work-life balance. The Employee Health & Wellbeing software suite was build on the foundation of SAP’s decades-long experience in business software solutions for diverse industries, and can be used either as a single solution or as a full enterprise intelligence solution.

 

5) The Functionality and the Major Benefits of SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing

 

SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing allows D&S organizations to easily monitor, analyze and improve the wellbeing and health of its workforce. The software solution, as the below illustration shows, is thereby easily integrable into existing systems and is also connectable with diverse other databases, such as HR systems. For measuring the health and wellbeing data of the individual employees, the application uses regular surveys, that are directly transferred into the application. The user then has a data collection of health data for everyone in the case management, which enables him to analyze and monitor it in detail. As a result, the organization can initiate individual mitigation and treatment plans for each employee. These action plans are highly customizable and can for example entail measures, such as medical support, trainings, the assignment of other tasks, vacation, the use of different work equipment or reduced working hours.

Figure 5: The high-level process behind SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing

 

Our SAP Solution for Employee Health & Wellbeing entails the following helpful characteristics and features for organizational health management:

One Single Platform for Wellbeing and Health Management

Our application allows you to manage all health and wellbeing issues centrally with one single, intuitive platform.

 

A Digital, Data-Driven Approach

SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing allows you to completely digitize your health management and make related decisions on the foundation of reliable data.

 

The Provision of Customizable Forms and Surveys for Measuring Wellbeing

Create highly customizable surveys and forms for successfully measuring the wellbeing of your workforce and make use of peer-based triggers.

 

A Single View of Employee’s Wellbeing

Monitor the wellbeing of each individual employee at a glance, with the ability of integrating employee data from your existing systems. Also easily provide access to the data to authorized groups, such as doctors or physical therapists.

Figure 6: Effective performance diagnostics with SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing

 

Efficient Human Performance Tracking

Profit from the ability to track the wellbeing of your employees, even of those that are operating in physically demanding environments. Easily integrate all available data sources like, for instance, from medical records over input from coaches to real-time performance data.

 

Figure 7: Holistic wellbeing tracking

 

Easy Integration of 3rd Party Applications

Easily integrate 3rd party applications and related services into the application.

 

Access to Comprehensive Case Management Functions

Make use of an advanced access management solution and easily enable cross agency collaboration.

 

Advanced Functions for Risk Analysis

Perform detailed risk analyses and trigger interventions based on user-defined indicators.

 

Discover how Activities Affect your Employees

SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing allows organizations to understand how certain day-to-day activities influence the wellbeing and health of their staff.

Figure 8: User-friendly case management with SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing

 

As the above benefits show, SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing is a truly holistic health management solutions, that not only allow you to perform better health management, but also to better care for each of your individual staff members. This way your organization can raise the employee satisfaction, better prevent the workforce from injuries and diseases, but also decrease absences and labor turnover. This, of course, also leads to significant cost savings, especially when considering the above-mentioned costs that can arise from fluctuation and health problems. Furthermore, SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing can also be an effective tool for the retainment of experienced employees with individual needs or for attracting new employees.

 

6) A Role Model for Effective Employee Wellbeing Management: The Australian Police Forces

 

The Australian Police Forces successfully integrated SAP Employee Health and Wellbeing into their organization. The software solution enabled the D&S organization to record, manage and report all health and wellbeing data with one single platform. In addition, SAP Wellbeing allowed the law enforcement organization to integrate much more health-related processes than initially expected. For example, it was even possible to build individual health management systems for the organization’s police dogs and horses.

Figure 9: SAP Wellbeing is even extendable to police dogs

 

All in all, the implementation of SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing allowed the Australian Police Forces to:

--> Reduce employee churn by up to 10%

--> Lower HR costs by up to 8%

--> Increase employee engagement by up to 20%

--> Improve HR reporting by up to 10%

 

7) Why Wellbeing Should not be Ignored by Employers

 

Although there are many ways how companies and organizations can theoretically manage organizational health and wellbeing, this blog post has hopefully highlighted what the advantages of a scalable and comprehensive software solutions, like SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing, are. The software suite not only allows organizations to manage health matters for every employee with one single solution, but also improves the wellbeing and health of your staff, which in turn also raises organizational performance measurably.  

 

Additional Information

 

  • SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing Video: Let's Talk - Public Services Wellbeing (sap.com)
  • Summary of SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing: 4 Page Executive Summary
  • L0 Presentation for SAP Employee Health & Wellbeing: L0 Deck

 

References

 

Health, Performance and Wellbeing in the Army. (2024). The British Army.

Mental health support for the UK armed forces. (2023, November). UK Government.

Wellbeing vans. (2024). Oscar Kilo.

Injury Prevention. (2024). HPRC.

Mental health. (2024). The Fire Fighters Charity.

Wellbeing Overview. (2023). City of London Police.

Predicting 36-Month Attrition in the U.S. Military: A Comparison Across Service Branches. (2020, April). RAND.

4th Annual Study Shows Worsening EMS Turnover. (2022, October). American Ambulance Association.

Burned out: pressures are taking a toll on wildfire fighters. (2023, August). Canadian Firefighter Magazine.

entry.title: Setup Time Optimizer query (Non-PPDS)
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am looking to introduce the use of Setup Groups and the Setup Matrix alongside CM25 and the Setup Time Optimization dispatch control for the Production Schedulers within a business. The workcenter only has one capacity category, and it is set for finite scheduling (as is the strategy)...

Just as a point of note, I have also created Setup Groups, assigned them to the recipe and maintained the setup matrix, so the information should be available for the system to calculate the best option.

I appreciate any knowledge someone might have on this
Many thanks!

entry.title: Top SAP PLM Sessions You Cannot Miss at SAP Sapphire Orlando 2024!
entry.content:
Bring out the best in your business with SAP Product Lifecycle Management at Sapphire 2024.

SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference in Orlando is starting soon, and we are excited to share some details about the Product Lifecycle Management presence at this event in Orlando, Florida, on June 3rd-5th.

We are proud to share, that leading companies like Coty Inc. and Huntsmann Crop. will present their experiences improving their business with SAP PLM software.


Check out the list of Top Sessions  we’ve compiled below:
Build your individual agenda before you go with the recommended sessions:
 
 
SCM161 – June 4th, 11:00

How Huntsman achieved effective capital project management in the cloud

Huntsman Corporation followed a stage-gate methodology for managing its capital projects using the SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management package. Explore how the chemical products manufacturer gained business value by using the cloud-based package to automate workflows and manage project budgeting, forecasting, and reporting.

Speaker: Arend Jan Zeeuw,  Global Capital Coordinator for Polyurethanes  Huntsman International LLC
 
 
SCM101 – June 4th, 11:00

Amplify your product development with SAP Product Lifecycle Management

Digitalize product development and innovate sustainable products faster with SAP Product Lifecycle Management (SAP PLM) solutions. We look at how innovations in SAP PLM support your journey with RISE with SAP and explore how the Joule copilot helps you harness AI to transform formulation processes.

Speaker: Sean Laughlin, SAP Solution Manager Digital Products & Projects


SCM163 – June 4th, 3:00

Taking formulation processes to the next level with SAP PLM at Coty

Join Coty Inc. to hear about its transformative journey to SAP S/4HANA. We also explore how the beauty company used SAP Product Lifecycle Management solutions to optimize formulation processes and consider the impact on product-centric teams.

Speaker: Suzanne Galluccio, VP of IT Commercial and R&D, Coty Inc.
 

SCM158 – June 5th, 11:00

Digital thread in SAP PLM for process industries

Discover how SAP PLM solutions help you create a digital thread for recipe design. We look at collaborative capabilities during the design and formulation process, augmentation of recipe designs with unified operational data, and synchronization across design-to-consume business processes.

Speaker: Rakesh Gandhi, SAP VP Strategic Partnerships – Industry 4.0, Digital Supply Chain
 

SCM156 – June 4th, 11:00

Digital thread in SAP PLM for discrete industries


SAP Product Lifecycle Management (SAP PLM) solutions support the creation of a digital thread and contextualized visualization of final designs. We explore collaborative capabilities during the design process, augmentation of product designs with a single operational data model, and synchronization across design-to-operate business processes.

Speakers: Rakesh Gandhi, SAP VP Strategic Partnerships – Industry 4.0, Digital Supply Chain

 

SCM137 - June 4th, 1:30

Expand your business with a risk-resilient and AI-enabled supply chain

Grow and protect your business with a data-driven, digital supply chain to connect your enterprise with the outside world. This enables you to connect supply chain processes with your business imperatives, contextualizing decisions by linking operational and business data together and collaborating with your trading partners digitally.

Speakers: 

  • Martin Barkman, SAP, SVP, Product Marketing, Supply Chain, Finance, and ERP AI
  • Dominik Metzger, SAP, Head of Engineering, SAP Digital Supply Chain


121 - All days

SAP Demo Station | Open During Show Floor Hours

Elevate design data and processes with SAP PLM


With SAP Product Lifecycle Management (SAP PLM), you can build a risk-resilient supply chain and define, develop, deliver, and manage innovative, sustainable, and compliant products. Supported by AI, you can connect processes, make better design decisions, and collaborate beyond your enterprise to win the race to market.

 

ASUG862 - June 3rd, 8:00 - 12:00

Realize Your Supply Chain Potential with SAP Business AI

Although AI is not new to supply chains, the amount of progress and innovation over the past few years has been enormous in terms of what is now practical and available. This seminar will cover the integration of AI in SAP´s Digital Supply Chain solutions based on our three core principles: Relevant, Reliable, Responsible. Attendees will gain access to experts and see firsthand how to synchronize operations and maximize efficiency with predictive maintenance, plan customer demand reliably with AI-powered demand forecasting and improve quality with intelligent anomaly detection through visual inspection with SAP Joule. Key Takeaways: understand how to leverage AI in your supply chain processes, learn how AI algorithms help your business enhance demand forecasting accuracy, optimize inventory levels, and improve end-to-end supply and demand visibility in real -time, experience how AI can streamline logistics and ensure route optimization, reducing costs, improving delivery efficiency, and lowering emissions, see how warehouse operations benefit from AI-powered robotics and automation, and ensure that AI technology is not perceived as a replacement for job functions but rather designed to make them more effective.

All ASUG pre-conference seminars take place on Monday, June 3. Additional fees for these seminars apply. You may purchase during the registration process or in your registration record.


534 - Partner booth

LeverX Inc.

LeverX was founded in 2003 as a company specializing in SAP solution implementation and customization. As an SAP gold partner and global strategic supplier, LeverX has delivered over 950 successful projects for 800+ customers, helping them maximize their investments in SAP solutions. Its primary mission is to deliver full-cycle technology consulting, development, and implementation services to businesses for their successful business transformation. LeverX's deep expertise is based on 20+ years of successful cooperation with SAP as a preferred vendor.

 

306 - Partner booth

Siemens Digital Industries Software

Siemens Digital Industries Software helps organizations of all sizes digitally transform using software, hardware, and services from the Siemens Xcelerator business platform. Siemens's software and the comprehensive digital twin enable companies to optimize their design, engineering, and manufacturing processes to turn today's ideas into the sustainable products of the future. From chips to entire systems, from product to process, across all industries, Siemens Digital Industries Software is accelerating transformation.



It's time for putting your personal agenda  together, today. Check out the full Session Catalog here.

Stay updated on all of the latest announcement regarding Supply Chain Management by following us on LinkedIn SAP Supply Chain Management.

Find more SAP PLM Solutions and information on our  Website .

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - How to make a SAP Fiori app or GUI transaction available on your lau
entry.content:
Hi Jocelyn,

could you please advise about adaptation projects done on standard S/4 HANA fiori apps through BAS? For that we get new app ID and we need to configure new target mapping pointing to it. In which technical catalog we should put it? Gut feel says we should add it to custom technical catalog. But note "3074554 - How to Create a Tile for Adaptation Project? - SAPUI5" says this: "Create a new target mapping in the same catalog as the source application that was adapted" - so one would think we would need to create new target mapping in the SAP standard technical catalog?

Thank you,

Ivo

entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: Interaction record created if I click on end button crm web ui
entry.content: Hello, Were you able to fix the issue. We are also facing the same issue of creation of IR on clicking End button. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Custom MDK Client build fails
entry.content:
Hi,

I could finally build. The key was that I have moved the downloaded MDK client to my Documents directory and therefore for some reason it was not a wise move. When I moved it back to my Downloads directory, now it's working fine.
I think it is quite interesting.

BTW: I had to downgrade also NativeScript to the proper version.

Thank you for the support!
